WLCG #1001954 (id:1001954) Testing new tape library at PIC (LHCb)
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2026-03-03 (19d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-03-11
Conversation (10 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Alexander Rogovskiy  |  2026-03-03 10:19
Dear site admins, This ticket is to coordinate new tape library tests @PIC. As i understand (correct me if i am wrong), this week LHCb is expected to write some data to `  https://dcdoor01-test.pic.es:8446/pnfs/pic.es/data/lhcb/CTA   `. A few questions: 1. Would that be OK to copy the data from production PIC SE  webdav-lhcbt1.pic.es  (via https)? 2. How much data should we copy? 3. When should we start? Best Regards, Alex
#2 Reply 1  |  Elena Planas  |  2026-03-03 17:54
Hi Alex, Sorry for the delay. We were doing some late tests and the system was not ready till now. Answering to your questions: 1. Yes, Production is perfect as source, and the endpoint provided is https. 2. There is a buffer of 127TB to write data in front of the tape, but you could copy more data. 3. Now the system is ready to accept data. Best Regards, Elena
#3 Reply 2  |  Alexander Rogovskiy  |  2026-03-04 10:17
Hi  Elena, Thanks! I've just (re)started the tests, transfers should start soonish. Best Regards, Alex
#4 Reply 3  |  Alexander Rogovskiy  |  2026-03-04 14:03
Hi  Elena, The transfers started, but they are all failing with, e.g. [1]: ``` INFO Wed, 04 Mar 2026 11:29:18 +0100; Davix: PerformanceMarker: failure: Failed to select pool: diskCacheV111.services.space.SpaceException: Access latency conflicts with access latency defined by default space reservation. ``` Could you please have a look? Best Regards, Alex [1]  https://fts-lhcb-005.cern.ch:8449/var/log/fts3/transfers/2026-03-04/webdav-lhcbt1.pic.es__dcdoor01-test.pic.es/2026-03-04-1029__webdav-lhcbt1.pic.es__dcdoor01-test.pic.es__253686662__0c5ae3ba-17b4-11f1-a9e4-e24dd15c0b06
#5 Reply 4  |  Elena Planas  |  2026-03-04 14:39
Hi Alex, There was defined a write token, inherited from production, when we recreated the path. Now we've removed it and the transfers should work. Sorry for the inconvenience. Cheers, Elena
#6 Reply 5  |  Alexander Rogovskiy  |  2026-03-05 13:55
Hi  Elena, Thanks! After the fix there were some successful uploads to the test SE. However, all of them failed during archival stage (e.g. when the file has already been transferred and FTS is just polling it until it is copied to tapes)  due to issues with tape rest API query. E.g. from [1] we got ``` ARCHIVING [5] [Tape REST API] Failed to query /.well-known/wlcg-tape-rest-api: curl error (18): Transferred a partial file ``` Could you please have a look? Best Regards, Alex [1]  https://fts3-lhcb.cern.ch:8449/fts3/ftsmon/#/job/6792dd34-187f-11f1-93cb-fa163e8e0048
#7 Reply 6  |  Elena Planas  |  2026-03-05 19:00
Hi Alex, The json file which provides the information to the query of well-know/wlcg-tape-rest-api was not defined correctly an that's why you received this error. I've already created it and checked that the  https://dcdoor01-test.pic.es:8446/.well-known/wlcg-tape-rest-api is responding correctly the endpoint to the Tape-rest-api Sorry for the delay in correcting it. Cheers Elena

Las week tests were stopped to drain the requests and allow the drives firmware update needed. @Alex: now the firmware is updaded and we can restart the tests, than you!

WLCG #1002126 (id:1002126) Upgrade your HTCondorCE endpoints to 24.0.x series (IN2P3-CC)
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2026-03-19 (3d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-03-19
Conversation (5 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Alessandro Paolini  |  2026-03-19 14:13
Dear site admins, The HTCondorCE v23 series (and older) became unsopported and the endpoints running it should be either decommissioned or upgraded to 24.0.x series. You received this ticket either because you provide at least one HTCondorCE endpoint out of support or because you provide HTCondorCE endpoint(s) but we couldn't determine the version by looking into the BDII. If you are running a supported version of HTCondor, please let us know which one is, make sure that the endpoints are properly published into the BDII (which will make it easier to carry on activities like this one), and then close the ticket. Instead, if you are running an unsupported version, we ask you to upgrade it as soon as possible. In the UMD repository you can find HTCondor-CE 24.0.2 and HTCondor 24.0.14, which is the minimum version that we recommend. Please check the full release notes of the 24.0.x series ( https://htcondor.readthedocs.io/en/latest/version-history/lts-versions-24-0.html ) and pay attention to the differences between v23.0.x and v24.0.x in terms of settings and features (for example the different syntax used for the SSL mapping). Please read carefully the documentation before the upgrade: all the changes with the upgrade must be applied manually, in particular the changes to the new syntax for the SSL mapping. The quick configuration guide for HTCondor24 created by WLCG can be useful for the upgrade process: https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/view/LCG/MiniHTCv24EL9 Thanks for your collaboration, EGI Operations
#2 Reply 1  |  Alessandro Paolini  |  2026-03-19 15:33
Hi Vanessa, actually I found the following CE with an unsupported version: -  cccondorcm01.in2p3.fr : 9.0.20 -  cccondorcm02.in2p3.fr : 23.10.1 They are registered in GOCDB and the version information is published in the BDII. Cheers, Alessandro

If you manage to configure the infoprovider and publish the CE in the bdii, it would be helpful. Cheers, Alessandro
WLCG #1001216 (id:1001216) IN2P3 missmatch with DFC, request for permission updates to folders
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2025-11-22 (120d ago)  |  Updated: 2025-12-01
Conversation (8 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Sophie King  |  2025-11-22 17:15
Hi Yvan First off, apologies for not following up on this ticket, I took quite a bit of holiday this summer and this fell by the wayside. https://helpdesk.ggus.eu/#ticket/zoom/3614 As reported here, I seem to bump into cases semi-regularly where a file is registered in the DFC at IN2P3, but the file doesn't actually exist there. I have found this for both disk and tape (and for tape, I believe it is impossible for the user to delete files). It is hard to debug, as the issue is generally only noticed quite a while after the files have been uploaded. I don't know if you have any suggestions? An example file is $ dirac-dms-lfn-replicas / t2k.org/nd280/production008/validation/V13/pc2/ND280/00016000_00016999/anal/oa_nd_spl_00016911-0012_ruxy4ofgca6e_anal_000_14.35-kekcc-bsdv01_2.root Replica Lookup Time: 0.20 seconds LFN                                                                                                                                                  StorageElement    URL ========================================================================================================================================================================== / t2k.org/nd280/production008/validation/V13/pc2/ND280/00016000_00016999/anal/oa_nd_spl_00016911-0012_ruxy4ofgca6e_anal_000_14.35-kekcc-bsdv01_2.root IN2P3-CC-XRD-disk davs:// ccxrdrli04.in2p3.fr:1097/xrootd/in2p3.fr/disk/t2k.org/t2k.org/nd280/production008/validation/V13/pc2/ND280/00016000_00016999/anal/oa_nd_spl_00016911-0012_ruxy4ofgca6e_anal_000_14.35-kekcc-bsdv01_2.root where I get the eror ERROR {'Successful': {}, 'Failed': {'/ t2k.org/nd280/production008/validation/V13/pc2/ND280/00016000_00016999/anal/oa_nd_spl_00016911-0012_ruxy4ofgca6e_anal_000_14.35-kekcc-bsdv01_2.root ': "GError('[gfal2_stat][gfal_plugin_statG][gfal_http_stat] Result HTTP 404 : File not found  after 1 attempts', 2) GError('[gfal2_stat][gfal_plugin_statG][gfal_xrootd_statG] Failed to stat file (No such file or directory)', 2)"}} One thing th

CMS tickets (1)
CMS #1000981 (id:1000981) Locality UNAVAILABLE CNAF Tape
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2025-10-27 (146d ago)  |  Updated: 2025-11-19
Conversation (7 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Christos Emmanouil  |  2025-10-27 15:05
We noticed that's an increasing issue previous days to transfers targeting to CNAF tape. Files seems to making it into the buffer, although FTS is not able to get the locality. As a result, locality is marked as unavailable and FTS fails the transfer with the following message: ``` ARCHIVING [11] [Tape REST API] File locality reported as UNAVAILABLE ``` Here is an example:  https://fts3-cms.cern.ch:8449/fts3/ftsmon/#/job/a5c49af4-b333-11f0-8322-fa163e1db714?file=5220387371 FTS logs here:  https://fts-cms-009.cern.ch:8449/var/log/fts3/transfers/2025-10-27/eoscms.cern.ch__xfer-tape-cms.cr.cnaf.infn.it/2025-10-27-1255__eoscms.cern.ch__xfer-tape-cms.cr.cnaf.infn.it__5220387371__a5c49af4-b333-11f0-8322-fa163e1db714 Trying manually, I was able to retrieve the locality (using both my personal and rucio certificate): ``` $ gfal-xattr davs:// xfer-tape-cms.cr.cnaf.infn.it:8443/cmstape/store/data/Run2025G/EGamma0/RAW-RECO/EGMJME-PromptReco-v1/000/398/419/00000/83b8a4f5-9e01-4c55-b184-1854b45f87d8.root user.status ONLINE ``` Could you have a look in case there is an issue on your side? Thanks, Christos for DM
#2 Reply 1  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2025-10-28 10:09
Hi Christos, regarding this particular example you mentioned, we believe that what happens is the following: one of our StoRM WebDAV servers successfully handles the TPC from EOS at 13:55:32: 2025-10-27T13:55:32+01:00 [f9f4a29e5958aff86a1e3d512ddcca1f;job-id=a5c49af4-b333-11f0-8322-fa163e1db714;file-id=5220387371;retry=0] job-id:a5c49af4-b333-11f0-8322-fa163e1db714,file-id:5220387371,retry:0 INFO 2023053 --- [jetty-5207954] o.i.storm.webdav.tpc.TransferFilter : Pull third-party transfer completed: DONE. Source: https://eoscms-ns-ip700.cern.ch:443/eos/cms/tier0/store/data/Run2025G/EGamma0/RAW-RECO/EGMJME-PromptReco-v1/000/398/419/00000/83b8a4f5-9e01-4c55-b184-1854b45f87d8.root , Destination: /cmstape/store/data/Run2025G/EGamma0/RAW-RECO/EGMJME-PromptReco-v1/000/398/419/00000/83b8a4f5-9e01-4c55-b184-1854b45f87d8.root, Bytes transferred: 2383492487, Duration (msec): 106597, Throughput: 21 MB/sec, id: f9f4a29e5958aff86a1e3d512ddcca1f;job-id=a5c49af4-b333-11f0-8322-fa163e1db714;file-id=5220387371;retry=0 The TPC is successful, which is something you can see also in the FTS log: checksum is correct. 9 seconds later, FTS queries file locality, and such request goes to the StoRM Tape REST API endpoint, which is a different one wrt to the StoRM WebDAV server above. Apparently, the information on the attributes of that file has not been propagated to all the filesystem nodes yet, and so we UNAVAILABLE as locality, which corresponds, in the log file of the service to the following line: Oct 27 13:55:41 tape-cms storm-tape[230526]: (2025-10-27 12:55:41) [ERROR ] The file /storage/gpfs_tsm_cms/cms/store/data/Run2025G/EGamma0/RAW-RECO/EGMJME-PromptReco-v1/000/398/419/00000/83b8a4f5-9e01-4c55-b184-1854b45f87d8.root appears lost, check stubbification and presence of user.storm.migrated xattr When you repeat the request manually, a few seconds after, the locality is correctly reported. Don't you think it would be best to adjust the workflow client-si
#3 Reply 2  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2025-10-30 14:20
Hi Christos,  thanks for the investigation.  We attach a plot of traffic OUT of the servers handling CMS filesystem, and you can see that on Oct 27th around 1-2pm, there actually was a spike of traffic. However, nothing particularly worrying. Given you'll contact the FTS team, we stress again that it would be best to avoid such unnecessary retries, and also avoid locality checking to assess the status of a TPC. Thanks for understanding, Lucia
#4 Reply 3  |  Christos Emmanouil  |  2025-10-30 13:50
Hi Lucia, Thanks for the explanation. That was really helpful. After looking into several cases from the Rucio side, I noticed that transfers were marked as completed after the first attempt [1]. It seems that the delay between StoRM and the Tape API isn’t constant, so this can likely be considered a non-critical issue. However, this state still adds unnecessary load to the site and FTS due to redundant retries. In some cases, this could cause problems. As far as I know, if a file doesn’t make it to tape before Rucio retries, the file will be deleted from the buffer and FTS will restart the process from scratch. If the delay still persist, this could result in an increasing number of unnecessary retries and potentially reduce overall transfer efficiency. Could we check whether this issue appears when the site is under heavy load? I can also try reducing the number of concurrent transfers that FTS submits to the site. In any case, I’ll contact the FTS team. Perhaps accepting UNAVAILABLE during archive polling could help in such cases. Thanks, Christos [1] https://monit-opensearch.cern.ch/dashboards/goto/76fa1bfcade6ffc4ba0c57edf1bdec46
#5 Reply 4  |  Christos Emmanouil  |  2025-11-12 11:21
Just placing the relevant JIRA ticket: https://its.cern.ch/jira/browse/CMSDM-362
#6 Reply 5  |  Christos Emmanouil  |  2025-11-13 11:18
Following FTS response; We should investigate the reason UNAVAILABLE is reported. As FTS reports, this is a state that should be reported in case of issues: https://its.cern.ch/jira/browse/CMSDM-362 I also managed to find a reference document that clearly describes the behavior (pages: 23 - 25): https://cernbox.cern.ch/pdf-viewer/public/vLhBpHDdaXJSqwW/WLCG%20Tape%20REST%20API%20reference%20document.pdf From what I understand, the delay of propagating information to the nodes can not clearly explain the  UNAVAILABLE locality.
#7 Reply 6  |  Francesco Giacomini  |  2025-11-13 14:07
From the point of view of the conformance to the Tape REST API, StoRM Tape should respond LOST, because at the moment of the request the file appears as a stub, meaning that the disk file has no contents, and it doesn't have the extended attribute that tells that it is on tape. This is what is reported in the log pasted above by Lucia. In practice, however, it returns UNAVAILABLE, not to scare the client too much :-) I don't see other options here and in fact UNAVAILABLE seems the perfect value for a transitory situation, which, for a distributed filesystem like GPFS, can certainly happen, since information needs some time to propagate, especially if the load is high.
WLCG tickets (4)
WLCG #1002105 (id:1002105) INFN-T1: failures on tape archive (archive monitor)
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2026-03-18 (4d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-03-19
Conversation (2 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Fabio Luchetti  |  2026-03-18 15:30
Dear site admins, Following up from <  https://its.cern.ch/jira/browse/ATLDDMOPS-5797   | Enable archive monitoring across all tape storages > , we notice a high number of failures as destination for the INFN-T1_MCTAPE endpoint (where the archive monitoring feature was enabled, while it wasn't on DATATAPE so far). For convenience, you can find the related plots and reports in the DDM Transfer dashoard at  https://monit-grafana.cern.ch/goto/bfmhAZ5vR?orgId=17  , specifically in the Transfer Plots, Errors, and Details panels. The most common error (40k+) reads like "ARCHIVING [11] [Tape REST API] File locality reported as UNAVAILABLE". This leads to repeated transfer attempts and, while investigating a few replicas, I noticed the we can end up with unnecessary duplicate replicas of the same file. For example (note the _1773763209 timestamp suffix): ``` # rucio list-file-replicas --rses INFN-T1_MCTAPE --pfns mc23_13p6TeV:AOD.46297565._002741.pool.root.1 davs:// xfer-tape-atlas.cr.cnaf.infn.it:8443/atlas-tape/atlasmctape/mc23_13p6TeV/AOD/e8592_e8586_s4369_s4370_r16083_r15970/mc23_13p6TeV.700781.Sh_2214_Wmunu_maxHTpTV2_CFilterBVeto.merge.AOD.e8592_e8586_s4369_s4370_r16083_r15970_tid46297565_00/AOD.46297565._002741.pool.root.1_1773763209 # curl -X POST --cert $X509_USER_PROXY --capath /etc/grid-security/certificates -H "Content-Type: application/json" https://tape-atlas.cr.cnaf.infn.it:8443/api/v1/archiveinfo  --data '{"paths":["/atlas-tape/atlasmctape/mc23_13p6TeV/AOD/e8592_e8586_s4369_s4370_r16083_r15970/mc23_13p6TeV.700781.Sh_2214_Wmunu_maxHTpTV2_CFilterBVeto.merge.AOD.e8592_e8586_s4369_s4370_r16083_r15970_tid46297565_00/AOD.46297565._002741.pool.root.1"]}' | jq  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed 100   508  100   263  100   245    903    841 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--  1739 [  {    "path": "/atlas-tape/atlasmctape/m
#2 Reply 1  |  Andrea Rendina  |  2026-03-19 12:42
Hi Fabio, thanks a lot for the notification. We figured out that the problem is strictly related to the proper management of the extended attributes of the files on the tape buffer: there is a brief window of time during which the files can be in the "UNAVAILABLE" status. This is quite normal in our opinion because there is always a migration period during which the files are in an in-transit state. We are evaluating some possible improvements and fixes, and we'll let you know about them. However, with respect to the file you mentioned, we tried to look at its history. The file was written on March 17th at 6:40: pull third-party transfer completed: DONE. Source: https://se1.farm.particle.cz:443/atlas/atlasdatadisk/rucio/mc23_13p6TeV/b4/bd/AOD.46297565._002741.pool.root.1?copy_mode=pull , Destination: /atlas-tape/atlasmctape/mc23_13p6TeV/AOD/e8592_e8586_s4369_s4370_r16083_r15970/mc23_13p6TeV.700781.Sh_2214_Wmunu_maxHTpTV2_CFilterBVeto.merge.AOD.e8592_e8586_s4369_s4370_r16083_r15970_tid46297565_00/AOD.46297565._002741.pool.root.1, Bytes transferred: 7243335889, Duration (msec): 207755, Throughput: 33 MB/sec but then you looked at the locality about 6 hours later, 4 times, with an interval of 10 minutes: Mar 17 12:36:37 Mar 17 12:46:37 Mar 17 12:56:37 Mar 17 13:06:37 So, some questions naturally arise. Can you confirm the time between the completed transfer and the first /archiveinfo attempt? Is it possible to configure or extend it up to 24 hours? Or were the observed 6 hours due to a certain load, or queue, on the FTS servers? How many times can the Tape REST API report an error or the UNAVAILABLE status before FTS declares the transfer as failed and retries? Can you extend the time between two /archiveinfo requests from 10 minutes to 1 hour? Have you considered avoiding the retry behaviour where a timestamp is appended to the file name, and instead simply retry transferring the same file? Thanks a lot, Andrea
WLCG #1002062 (id:1002062) INFN-T1 transfer, deletion and staging failures
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2026-03-12 (11d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-03-19
Conversation (12 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Ngoc Khanh Vu  |  2026-03-12 01:40
Dear site admins, I would like to report that the site INFN-T1, especially INFN-T1_DATATAPE has been failing as a source for transfers and staging from this site endpoint, with ~4.13K transfer failures over the past 6h. The most common errors are:  STAGING [22] [Tape REST API] Stage call failed: HTTP 502 : Unexpected server error: 502 : <html> <head><title>502 Bad Gateway</PATH <body> <center><h1>502 Bad Gateway</PATH <hr><center>nginx/PATH </body> </html>   TRANSFER ERROR: Copy failed (3rd pull). Last attempt: copy HTTP 500 : Unexpected server error: 500 The site has also shown a high rate of deletion failure (only ~50% deletion efficiency over the past 6h) Link:  https://monit-grafana.cern.ch/d/FtSFfwdmk/ddm-transfers?orgId=17&var-binning=%24__auto_interval_binning&var-groupby=src_experiment_site&var-activity=Analysis+Input&var-activity=Analysis+Output&var-activity=Data+Carousel+Analysis&var-activity=Data+Carousel+Production&var-activity=Data+Challenge&var-activity=Data+Consolidation&var-activity=Data+Rebalancing&var-activity=Deletion&var-activity=Express&var-activity=Functional+Test&var-activity=Production+Input&var-activity=Production+Output&var-activity=Recovery&var-activity=Staging&var-activity=T0+Export&var-activity=T0+Tape&var-activity=T0+Tape+Derived&var-activity=T0+Tape+RAW&var-activity=User+Subscriptions&var-activity=default&var-activity=T0+Recall&var-src_tier=0&var-src_tier=1&var-src_tier=2&var-src_country=All&var-src_cloud=All&var-src_site=INFN-T1&var-src_endpoint=All&var-src_token=All&var-columns=src_experiment_site&var-dst_tier=0&var-dst_tier=1&var-dst_tier=2&var-dst_country=All&var-dst_cloud=All&var-dst_site=All&var-dst_endpoint=All&var-dst_token=All&var-rows=dst_experiment_site&var-measurement=ddm_transfer&var-retention_policy=raw&var-include=&var-exclude=TEST%7CPPS%7CGRIDFTP%7CLAKE%7CAWS&var-exclude_es=All&var-include_es_dst=All&var-include_es_src=All&var-activity_disabled=Analysis+Input&var-activity_disabled=Data+Consolidation&var-activity

Hi again, concerning the specific transfer you mentioned, I checked the log files of our services. The recall process succeeded at  03:17:34 today: 03/12/26 03:17:34 tsm-hsm-7 yamssProcessRecall[2540341]: Recall of file /storage/gpfs_tsm_atlas/atlas/atlasdatatape/data17_13TeV/AOD/r13575_p5087/data17_13TeV.00338377.physics_BphysLS.merge.AOD.r13575_p5087_tid28874557_00/AOD.28874557._000032.pool.root.1 succeded the file is on buffer (it still is), and xfer-atlas has correctly performed a GET at  04:09:18  today : 2026-03-12T04:09:18+01:00 [a4f382aa3d998cb9da7448f727ddb6a5] 2001:1458:301:27::100:75 - "-" "GET /atlas-tape/atlasdatatape/data17_13TeV/AOD/r13575_p5087/data17_13TeV.00338377.physics_BphysLS.merge.AOD.r13575_p5087_tid28874557_00/AOD.28874557._000032.pool.root.1?copy_mode=pull HTTP/1.1" "xrootd-tpc/5.9.1" 200 1228974757 6.849  where you see all the  1228974757 bytes seem correctly transfered... I don't see a problem but please help me, lucia
#6 Reply 5  |  Petr Vokac  |  2026-03-13 00:52
Hi Luca, TLS communication hangs for some time / sometimes even timeout, e.g. following transfer gets stuck 3 times for quite some time (40s, 22s, 226s) and there is no packet loss. Are (all?) your servers somehow overloaded? Still it is weird that transfers gets stuck even during TLS handshake $ curl -s -v --capath /etc/grid-security/certificates -L -I -H "Authorization: Bearer $TSRC" "$SRC" 2>&1 | timestamp Mar 13 01:16:42.011 0.007 0.007 * Trying 2001:760:4205:128::129:204:8443... Mar 13 01:16:42.039 0.034 0.027 * Connected to xfer-atlas.cr.cnaf.infn.it (2001:760:4205:128::129:204) port 8443 (#0) Mar 13 01:16:42.040 0.036 0.002 * ALPN, offering h2 Mar 13 01:16:42.040 0.036 0.000 * ALPN, offering http/1.1 Mar 13 01:16:42.048 0.043 0.008 * CAfile: /etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt Mar 13 01:16:42.048 0.043 0.000 * CApath: /etc/grid-security/certificates Mar 13 01:16:42.048 0.043 0.000 * TLSv1.0 (OUT), TLS header, Certificate Status (22): Mar 13 01:16:42.048 0.043 0.000 } [5 bytes data] Mar 13 01:16:42.048 0.043 0.000 * TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS handshake, Client hello (1): Mar 13 01:16:42.048 0.043 0.000 } [512 bytes data] Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.290 40.247 * TLSv1.2 (IN), TLS header, Certificate Status (22): Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.001 { [5 bytes data] Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 * TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Server hello (2): Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 { [122 bytes data] Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 * TLSv1.2 (IN), TLS header, Finished (20): Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 { [5 bytes data] Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 * TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS change cipher, Change cipher spec (1): Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 { [1 bytes data] Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 * TLSv1.2 (IN), TLS header, Unknown (23): Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 { [5 bytes data] Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 * TLSv1.3 (IN), TLS handshake, Encrypted Extensions (8): Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 { [25 bytes data] Mar 13 01:17:22.295 40.291 0.000 * TLSv1.2 (IN), TLS header,
#7 Reply 6  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-03-13 09:14
Hi Petr, thanks for debugging the issue. Servers are not overloaded, nor is the underlying filesystem. We have evidence that some nginx workers get stucked for some requests, which was not expected.  The StoRM developers are looking into this. Cheers, lucia
#8 Reply 7  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-03-17 08:50
Hi Petr and everybody, we've been looking into this issue together with StoRM developers, who identified a possible limitation in the current Nginx configuration. Hence, we modified the configuration in 4 out of 5 endpoints in the xfer-atlas.cr.cnaf.infn.it alias, leaving only one unchanged to further debug. "Unfortunately", traffic is now very low, so we do not see any issue at the moment, but we are waiting for you to "push the button" :-) Thanks for understanding, lucia
#9 Reply 8  |  Michal Svatos  |  2026-03-18 12:14
Hi, from what I can see, the traffic is rather stable since this ticket was submitted: #10 Reply 9  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-03-18 14:27
Hi, the investigation is still ongoing on our side and unfortunately we do not believe that the new configuration solved the issues reported here. Are you saying that ATLAS is not observing low efficiency even with a traffic similar to the one ATLAS generated last week, when this ticket was opened? Thanks for checking, lucia
#11 Reply 10  |  Michal Svatos  |  2026-03-18 14:53
Yes, according to that plot I linked, the failures stopped on 13th of March.
#12 Reply 11  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-03-19 10:32
Hi, we enabled asyncronous I/O in all the endpoints given our analysis suggests this improves the handling of GET requests. It would be great if you could increase the timeout for DELETE requests to 30 seconds, in order to avoid false negatives. Then, ok for us to close this ticket. Thanks for your help, lucia
WLCG #1001512 (id:1001512) Problem staging files
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2026-01-12 (69d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-03-16
Conversation (45 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-02-10 09:06
Hi Lucia. Any news on the 2 files ?  Thanks  Chris
#2 Reply 1  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-02-11 10:33
Hi Andrea, I will re-replicate the one with the bad checksum.  I am waiting for news regarding the damaged tape. I have another 800 files that had strange staging errors, so maybe they are on the same tape
#3 Reply 2  |  Andrea Rendina  |  2026-02-11 11:36
Hi Chris, thanks a lot for your feedback. We will update you as soon as we have any news. Could you also share some logs or the paths of the 800 files you mentioned? Cheers, Andrea
#4 Reply 3  |  Andrea Rendina  |  2026-02-10 11:46
Hi Chris, sorry for the late reply. Regarding the file with a checksum different from the EA one, you can decide how you would like to proceed. As for the other file, it is very likely that the tape is damaged and that the file is unrecoverable. If you are able to re-transfer it, that would be preferable. In the meantime, we are trying to recover it anyway, along with any other files that may be affected. Sorry for the inconvenience. Cheers, Andrea
#5 Reply 4  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-12 10:35
Hi, as you know we are currently restaging our 2025 dataset. However the progress are very slow at CNAF. Over the past week, less that 500 TB have been staged.  Also we observe quite a number of errors like  Error reason: STAGING [22] [Tape REST API] Stage call failed: HTTP 502 : Unexpected server error: 502 : <html> <head><title>502 Bad Gateway</title></head> <body> <center><h1>502 Bad Gateway</h1></center> <hr><center>nginx/1.26.3</center> </body> </html> ) Example file:   https://xfer-tape-lhcb.cr.cnaf.infn.it:8443/tape/lhcb/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326278/326278_00110000_0957.raw Can you please investigate ? We are aiming at finishing the full staging before the end of January. Many thanks !  Chris
#6 Reply 5  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-01-12 16:43
Hi Chris, we investigated the issue you reported. Unfortunately, in the last two days we had a problem with the tape drive orchestrator, which prevented LHCb from using more than one tape drive for staging. This was fixed in the morning, and we apologize for the incovenience. In any case, as you reported, the rate is lower than expected, given LHCb is currently using 4 tape drives for staging. For this reason, we are upgrading the firmware of the tape drives in our new IBM library, which should improve the situation. Also, we have dedicated one more tape drive to LHCb. Finally, for what concerns 502 errors, they are only a few, and not worrying according to StoRM developers. Unfortunately, as already noticed for CMS, FTS workflow seems to be so that, following a STAGE request, GET requests are issued every 10 minutes and as soon as one of such request gets an error (maybe temporary), the whole STAGE requests is cancelled, even if the file was being correctly processed by the backend. Hope to have better news soon, and we'll keep an eye on the staging rate for LHCb. Cheers, lucia
#7 Reply 6  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-13 10:51
Hi Lucia, It was better over night but it seems to have degraded again. Also the error rate is almost 50 %  in the last 6 hours. can you please check again ?  Thanks Chris
#8 Reply 7  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-13 16:41
Hi, any news ? There does not seem to be any file staged today. Thanks Chris
#9 Reply 8  |  Andrea Rendina  |  2026-01-14 10:25
Hi Chris, sorry for the late reply, but we are doing our best to improve the situation. Yesterday evening we enabled the NVMe pool also for the stages, and not only for the migrations from buffer to tape, but we are not seeing any improvement until this moment. As Lucia already wrote, a new tape drive firmware was released a few days ago, but to upgrade it we need to have the tape drives offline, even if it seems that the tape drives with the updated firmware work better. For this reason, we are temporarily stopping the tape operations in order to update the firmware on all the tape drives. Moreover, yesterday you reported that 0 files had been staged, whereas in our logs we see that 11629 have been successfully staged in the 24 hours of the 13th of January. This could also be related to the 502 errors, and the StoRM developers are still investigating, but the errors are very low. How many files are grouped into a stage by FTS+Dirac? 200? We will update you as soon as we have something new. Sorry for the inconvenience, Andrea
#10 Reply 9  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-14 15:54
Ciao Andrea Thanks for the update !  Regarding yesterday, I naively looked at roughly the "working day", and sure enough, there was nothing after 5am and nothing before 19:00 ! Hence my impression.  regarding the number of errors, I have to check with the FTS people because the monitoring has a problem, but I'd guess we saw around 800 of them.  FTS aggregates files into queries behind the scene, I'd need to check with them how they do that. Out of curiosity, why does it matter here ?  Cheers Chris
#11 Reply 10  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-01-14 15:56
Hi Chris, as we said, "FTS workflow seems to be so that, following a STAGE request, GET requests are issued every 10 minutes and as soon as one of such request gets a 502 error (maybe temporary), the whole STAGE requests is cancelled, even if the file was being correctly processed by the backend." This is why the size of the stage request matters...  Cheers, lucia
#12 Reply 11  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-19 15:51
Ciao Lucia, the FTS developers are going to try a test version to change the behavior of FTS with the 502 error They'll deploy it tomorrow morning  Cheers Chris
#13 Reply 12  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-01-20 08:43
Ciao Chris, great to hear that, and we really hope this will help decreasing the problems with staging. Cheers, lucia
#14 Reply 13  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-21 09:06
Ciao Lucia, There has been no occurrence of error 502 in the last few days. Do you have any idea why ?  The performance of the staging does seem to have improved. What have you changed ?  According to our FTS friends, the errors we saw the last day are of a different type: mysql> select reason from t_file where source_se = ' https://xfer-tape-lhcb.cr.cnaf.infn.it ' and file_state = 'FAILED' and finish_time > date_sub(utc_timestamp(), interval 1 day); +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | reason                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | +------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ | TRANSFER [5] TRANSFER ERROR: Copy failed (3rd pull). Last attempt: Transfer failure: Error fetching https://dm-s11-14-19.cr.cnaf.infn.it:8443/tape/lhcb/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326618/326618_00080001_0026.raw : 415 status code: 415, reason phrase: Unsupported Media Type | | TRANSFER [5] TRANSFER ERROR: Copy failed (3rd pull). Last attempt: Transfer failure: Error fetching https://dm-s11-14-25.cr.cnaf.infn.it:8443/tape/lhcb/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326563/326563_00050001_0525.raw : 415 status code: 415, reason phrase: Unsupported Media Type | | TRANSFER [5] TRANSFER ERROR: Copy failed (3rd pull). Last attempt: Transfer failure: Error fetching https://dm-s11-1
#15 Reply 14  |  Andrea Rendina  |  2026-01-21 12:26
Hi Chris, thanks a lot for the feedback and for your work. It is very much appreciated. Regarding the 415 errors, in the last days we tried to stage in the files into the NVMe pool of the file system. Since this pool has a limited size, namely 72.5 TB, we had to decrease the pintime to 12 hours. Unfortunately, yesterday evening a large number of recalls — about 12k files — finished around the same time and it seems that, for a few of them (we do not see many of these unfortunate 415 status codes in our log files — don’t you agree?), 12 hours were not enough for FTS to submit the TPCs from the tape buffer to disk. For example, here you can see the history of a file for which StoRM WebDAV returned a 415 HTTP error: /tape/lhcb/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326563/326563_00050004_0403.raw This file was submitted to our tape backend on 01/19/26 at 21:07:25 and was successfully staged in on 01/20/26 at 13:13:18 [...]: Recall of file /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326563/326563_00050004_0403.raw succeeded After 12 hours, the file was removed from the buffer, becoming a stub file, as expected: 01/21/26 01:17:00 [...]: Migration of file /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326563/326563_00050004_0403.raw succeeded However, one minute later FTS tried to transfer it: 2026-01-21T01:18:15+01:00 [...] - "CN=Robot: LHCb offline productions,CN=693025,CN=lbprods,OU=Users,OU=Organic Units,DC=cern,DC=ch" "GET /tape/lhcb/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326563/326563_00050004_0403.raw HTTP/1.1" 415 1820 7 Eventually, the file was resubmitted again for staging in on 01/21/26 at 01:57:14. Given that we now redirect the stage-ins into the other pools, we have enlarged the pintime to 48 hours. This should be enough to avoid the 415 errors. However, do you have an expected time between a successful stage-in and the start of the buffer-to-disk TPC? Is there also a timeout in FTS
#16 Reply 15  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-22 13:25
Hi Andrea thanks for the detailed answer !  The FTS developers have put a fix to handle the 502 error, but it did not show up.  The performance had indeed really improved, but there starts being holes in the plot again. Is that expected ?  Thanks Chris
#17 Reply 16  |  Andrea Rendina  |  2026-01-23 14:38
Hi Chris, apologies for this late reply. Yesterday, from approximately 4am to 8pm, two tape drives were practically stuck. This is a known issue that we reported to the vendor more than four months ago, and there is still an open ticket about this: it sometimes happens that the TS1170 tape drives run at a throughput of only a few hundred KB/s for an indefinite period of time. Unfortunately, it seems that the new firmware we installed last week did not solve this issue. In any case, since yesterday evening at around 8pm, the situation has returned to a stable state, and we are now seeing an average throughput of about 1.15 GB/s, with peaks above 1.6 GB/s, and less than 25k files to be staged still. Cheers, Andrea
#18 Reply 17  |  Andrea Rendina  |  2026-01-26 10:14
Hi Chris, it seems the staging activity is over (and January is not over yet, so great). Do you expect to submit other requests? Can we close the ticket? Cheers, Andrea
#19 Reply 18  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-26 11:33
Ciao Andrea I still see 1598 files waiting to be staged. Don't you see them ?  Besides these, yes, we are done ! :-) Thanks a lot
#20 Reply 19  |  Andrea Rendina  |  2026-01-26 12:04
Ciao Chris, thanks a lot for the feedback. Actually, our tape backend has not processed any stages since yesterday evening at 9 pm until about 15 minutes ago, but now we are taking charge of about 6k files, distributed across 87 cartridges. Is this expected?
#21 Reply 20  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-26 13:36
yes, I've manually kicked for retry the files that had failed earlier with the 502 errors

If not, I’m afraid you’ll need to resubmit the stages again or provide us with the list of missing files. Thanks again for the collaboration, and sorry for the inconvenience, Andrea
#26 Reply 25  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-30 09:44
Hi Andrea It indeed is finished. HOWEVER, we found a problem and we will need to restage some more. Are you ready ? :-D
#27 Reply 26  |  Andrea Rendina  |  2026-01-30 09:49
Hi Chris, Yes, please go ahead.
#28 Reply 27  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-30 11:52
I started. I expect about 300TB to be recalled  Thanks !
#29 Reply 28  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-02-13 14:24
Hi Andrea, After a 3rd retry, all the 800 files went through. Not sure why they were failing, I was just having the obscure "failed staging" We are just left with the one broken tape I believe !  Thanks Chris
#30 Reply 29  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-01-31 12:07
Hi Andrea.  Everything has been submitted. For the time being, I still see this "hole" patterns (see attached) Is it a "warm up" effect, or is there something to tune ?  Thanks Chris
#31 Reply 30  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-02-02 09:37
Hi Chris, LHCb has been constantly staging in files in the last three days, no holes in our plot, which is attached. I guess what you are seeing is the usual FTS behavior, ie handling buffer-to-disk TPCs only after all the files in a "stage-in request bunch" have been successfully recalled to disk. This obviously raises some issues concerning the pin time, as Andrea was asking above: " do you have an expected time between a successful stage-in and the start of the buffer-to-disk TPC? Is there also a timeout in FTS between these two events? Do you have any recommendation for the pintime? " Cheers, lucia
#32 Reply 31  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-02-02 10:21
Ah ok I see. I will ask the FTS expert about this delay.
#33 Reply 32  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-02-02 13:48
So, according to our friendly experts in FTS, there is "in principle" no delay between the end of a staging, and the transfer out. "in principle" because we have a limit on that like (tape to disk) set to 50, which we probably set at some point back in time. However, they have checked, and at the moment,  there does not seem to be any pileup going on
#34 Reply 33  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-02-02 14:10
Hi Chris, and then how does it work? Is FTS checking locality file by file, without waiting for the whole stage request to be completed? If this is the case, why doesn't it start buffer-tape TPCs? In Andrea's example above, you can see one particular case we analysed there, where a stagein request for a file was submitted to our tape backend on 01/19/26 at 21:07:25, then the file was successfully staged-in the day after: 01/20/26 at 13:13:18 [...]: Recall of file /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326563/326563_00050004_0403.raw succeeded and then it stood in buffer for 12 hours, before being stubbified 01/21/26 01:17:00 [...]: Migration of file /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326563/326563_00050004_0403.raw succeeded and unfortunately only one minute later FTS tried to transfer it to disk 2026-01-21T01:18:15+ and failed. How is this possible if "in principle" there is no delay between the end of a staging and the transfer out? Also, we are pretty sure we recently observed such a delay (and holes in plot) for CMS as well. Cheers, lucia
#35 Reply 34  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-02-03 15:18
I'll add here the answer to your question from Joao (FTS) In the plot on the right side we can see that at 1AM the number of submitted transfers on the link https://xfer-tape-lhcb.cr.cnaf.infn.it → https://xfer-lhcb.cr.cnaf.infn.it jumped significantly from 0 to eventually peaking at 5K .. This is probably because at this moment they mounted a tape and started writing to their local disk area at a very high throughput The plot on the left side shows that FTS started immediately transferring files out of https://xfer-tape-lhcb.cr.cnaf.infn.it but quickly reached the configured limit of 50 parallel transfers (which in this case seems to map to 2GiB/second of throughput ) In this case, yes, there's a pile-up of transfers waiting to be transferred out of their disk buffer area https://xfer-lhcb.cr.cnaf.infn.it .. but it's just because the pipe they are using to write in is bigger than the one they are using to read out.. Also, to summarize the discussion I just had with Andrea (CNAF) and Steve (FTS): there may be no reason anymore to cap the transfers between CNAF tape to CNAF Disk to 50, so let's increase a bit.
#36 Reply 35  |  Lucia Morganti  |  2026-02-03 15:29
Ok, we'll see what happens when increasing such cap. Thanks everybody, lucia
#37 Reply 36  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-02-05 09:04
Hi, the staging is now mostly done but 3 files repeatedly fail ( https://fts3-lhcb.cern.ch:8449/fts3/ftsmon/#/job/b32334e2-01dc-11f1-a34c-fa163e0a22e3 https://fts3-lhcb.cern.ch:8449/fts3/ftsmon/#/job/b3e83e0e-01dc-11f1-8a8c-fa163e0a22e3 ) https://xfer-tape-lhcb.cr.cnaf.infn.it:8443/tape/lhcb/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/321844/321844_00140002_0319.raw https://xfer-tape-lhcb.cr.cnaf.infn.it:8443/tape/lhcb/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326223/326223_00080000_1121.raw These first 2 just say "failed staging" with no extra info That one claims it has a wrong checksum ( https://fts3-lhcb.cern.ch:8449/fts3/ftsmon/#/job/b6574572-01dc-11f1-a053-fa163e4e8fd9) https://xfer-tape-lhcb.cr.cnaf.infn.it:8443/tape/lhcb/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/327140/327140_00130000_0028.raw Can you please check ?

Hi Chris, so for these 3 files: / storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/321844/321844_00140002_0319.raw belongs to a tape which gave problems and errors. Our sysadmins are currently trying to re-read it and we'll let you know about it. /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326223/326223_00080000_1121.raw is not on our filesystem. /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/327140/327140_00130000_0028.raw has been correctly recalled on Feb  4th 16:50:51 Cheers, lucia
#39 Reply 38  |  Christophe Haen  |  2026-02-05 17:12
Ciao Lucia Thanks !  For /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/326223/326223_00080000_1121.raw that's a surprise but ok, I'll re-replicate it !  For  /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/327140/327140_00130000_0028.raw   what checksum does it have on your tape ? #42 Reply 41  |  Jan van Eldik  |  2026-02-24 15:24
Hi  Andrea Rendina  - do you have an update concerning that broken tape? Thanks, cheers, Jan
#43 Reply 42  |  Alessandro Cavalli  |  2026-02-24 15:43
Hi, we are working with IBM on tape problems occurring in some case with a number of tapes. One tape is LHCb and impacting your staging requests. At present all our tries to move the data on that tape to another one failed, and it's still 50% full. I can't tell if and when there will be good news on that, these problems are not yet understood in the investigation with IBM. One thing we could do meanwhile, if we provide you the list of the files remaining on the tape, you could check how many are available in other copies. Those could be invalidated here and transferred again at CNAF. Sorry for this incident let us know about this idea cheers Alessandro
#44 Reply 43  |  Alessandro Cavalli  |  2026-03-06 14:33
Hi, we decided to try selective ranges of recall, and we were lucky: most of the files of that tape are now recovered on other tapes. Total files:   3971 Recovered:   3871 Working:  100 the last 100 files are going slower, this seems to be the problematic part of the tape. I will keep you informed cheers Alessandro
#45 Reply 44  |  Alessandro Cavalli  |  2026-03-16 15:39
Hi, the recovery is almost done, but working on the last 9 files it's becoming almost certain that we'll lose them. These are the files that risk to be lost: /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/HLT2CALIB/LHCb/COLLISION25/321868/321868_00110000_0001.raw /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/321868/321868_00110011_0959.raw /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/321868/321868_00110011_0968.raw /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/TURCAL/LHCb/COLLISION25/321868/321868_00110002_0392.raw /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/TURCAL/LHCb/COLLISION25/323226/323226_00050000_0096.raw /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/323226/323226_00050002_0146.raw /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/323226/323226_00050002_0133.raw /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/323226/323226_00050002_0120.raw /storage/gpfs_lhcb/lhcb/tape/data/2025/RAW/FULL/LHCb/COLLISION25/323226/323226_00050002_0124.raw tomorrow I think I'll be ready to confirm it. The other  3971 - 9 =  3962  files on that tape should be fully available. cheers Alessandro
WLCG #681615 (id:1716) Request to deploy IPv6 on CEs and WNs at WLCG sites (INFN-T1)
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2025-01-29 (417d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-01-22
Conversation (13 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  GGUS Import Technical User  |  2025-01-29 09:59
GGUS ID: 164372 Last modifier: Andrea Sciaba Date: 2023-11-28 15:37:47 Subject: Request to deploy IPv6 on CEs and WNs at WLCG sites (INFN-T1) Ticket Type: USER CC: Status: assigned Responsible Unit: NGI_IT Issue type: Other Description: Dear Tier-1/Tier-2 Site Support, Please deploy dual-stack connectivity (IPv4+IPv6) on your computing services (computing elements and worker nodes) as soon as possible and by 30 June 2024 at the latest. This is in response to a new deployment plan for IPv6, mandated by the WLCG Management Board and the LHC experiments. For more details on the goal, the motivations and technical aspects, see https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/view/LCG/WlcgIpv6#IPv6Comp . Please note that switching off IPv4 is NOT requested nor recommended at this stage: any step in this direction should first be discussed with the LHC experiments you support and WLCG. Another purpose of this ticket is to track the status of this IPv6 deployment process at your site. As a first step  we ask you to answer this ticket as soon as possible with this information: your estimate of the timescale for the deployment; a few details about the steps required to fulfill the request; and to add comments to this ticket whenever progress has been made. In the unfortunate case it becomes evident that the deadline cannot be met, we would appreciate it if you could explain what are the obstacles and still give an estimate for the time of completion. This ticket will only be closed on successful testing conducted by the LHC VO(s) supported by your site and using a dedicated IPv6-only ETF instance running the experiment’s functional tests. For questions and requests for help you can contact the 'WLCG IPv6' support unit in GGUS.
#2 Reply 1  |  GGUS Import Technical User  |  2025-01-29 09:59
GGUS ID: 164372 Last modifier: Andrea Sciaba Date: 2024-06-06 08:34:17 Public Diary: Dear Daniele, yes, it is acceptable. Ciao, Andrea Internal Diary: Escalated this ticket to NGI_IT
#3 Reply 2  |  GGUS Import Technical User  |  2025-01-29 09:59
GGUS ID: 164372 Last modifier: Daniele Cesini Date: 2024-10-03 09:29:00 Public Diary: quick update on this: we completed the transition of the farm to the new datacenter, this also involved the inclusion of WNs instantiated on supercomputing resources at CINECA.(200WNs). We "see" this resources trough InfiniBand-Ethernet bridges, the SKYWAY by NVIDIA-Mellanox. Unfortunately, it resulted that this devices do not support IPv6 on the data interfaces (only on management interfaces). So it is impossible for us to configure all the farm in dual stack and we prefer not to have it enabled on just a fraction of the nodes (gpfs will be affected). We are pushing NVIDIA-Mellanox to release a firmware update to supper IPv6 on the data plane, but it seems that this will not happen shortly, so it is difficult to provide a new deadline for completing the task. Next iteration with NVIDIA on this topic will happen at SC25 in November. Kind Regards,    Daniele. Internal Diary: Escalated this ticket to NGI_IT
#4 Reply 3  |  GGUS Import Technical User  |  2025-01-29 09:59
GGUS ID: 164372 Last modifier: Carmelo Pellegrino Date: 2023-11-28 15:42:49 Status: in progress Responsible Unit: NGI_IT Public Diary: We are unable to perform this at the moment. Internal Diary: Escalated this ticket to NGI_IT
#5 Reply 4  |  GGUS Import Technical User  |  2025-01-29 09:59
GGUS ID: 164372 Last modifier: Carmelo Pellegrino Date: 2023-11-29 06:43:43 Public Diary: We are unable to perform this at the moment. Internal Diary: Escalated this ticket to NGI_IT

GGUS ID: 164372 Last modifier: Daniele Cesini Date: 2024-06-03 07:27:13 Public Diary: Dear Andrea, we will probably not manage to fulfill the request by the 30th of June. We are in the middle of the live transition to the new datacenter and we would prefer to configure IPv6 on the WNs when the transition will be completed in September. All other services (CEs, DM) are already in dual stack. Please let us know if this is acceptable. Kind Regards,     Daniele. Internal Diary: Escalated this ticket to NGI_IT

#2 Reply 1  |  Artem Petrosyan  |  2026-02-17 07:28
Hello Panos, Thanks for the link, centrally controlled testing suite is much better than exceptions for users. Worked smoothly: gfal-ls https://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/mc/SAM/GenericTTbar/AODSIM/CMSSW_9_2_6_91X_mcRun1_realistic_v2-v1/00000/A64CCCF2-5C76-E711-B359-0CC47A78A3F8.root https://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/mc/SAM/GenericTTbar/AODSIM/CMSSW_9_2_6_91X_mcRun1_realistic_v2-v1/00000/A64CCCF2-5C76-E711-B359-0CC47A78A3F8.root cd /tmp/ gfal-copy https://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/mc/SAM/GenericTTbar/AODSIM/CMSSW_9_2_6_91X_mcRun1_realistic_v2-v1/00000/A64CCCF2-5C76-E711-B359-0CC47A78A3F8.root . ... [DONE]  after 6s gfal-copy A64CCCF2-5C76-E711-B359-0CC47A78A3F8.root https://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/temp/sitetest/lalala.root Copying file:///tmp/A64CCCF2-5C76-E711-B359-0CC47A78A3F8.root   [DONE]  after 4s                                                                               gfal-ls https://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/temp/sitetest/lalala.root https://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/temp/sitetest/lalala.root gfal-rm https://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/temp/sitetest/lalala.root https://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/temp/sitetest/lalala.root DELETED gfal-ls https://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/temp/sitetest/lalala.root gfal-ls error: 2 (No such file or directory) - Result HTTP 404 : File not found  after 1 attempts Cheers, Artem
#3 Reply 2  |  Artem Petrosyan  |  2026-02-16 13:48
Dear colleagues, Stephan, thank you for configs. We've reviewed and changed our configuration in accordance with this document:  https://wlcg-authz-wg.github.io/wlcg-authz-docs/token-based-authorization/configuration/dcache/ Gfal ls, copy work well, deletion can not be checked from our side because I can not get scope storage.modify. #4 Reply 3  |  Panos Paparrigopoulos  |  2026-02-17 07:41
Thanks a lot Artem! That's great news!  I re-enabled tokens in the production RSE.  We will check that deletions are working in the next couple days and we'll close this ticket. Cheers, Pano
#5 Reply 4  |  Panos Paparrigopoulos  |  2026-02-16 15:17
Dear Artem, Thank you very much for investigating this. I can see successful deletions on the token-enabled _Test RSE since 07/02. If this correlates with your changes, it most likely means that tape deletions will also work on the production RSE. In any case, if you can share your IAM client ID with me, I can probably grant you elevated permissions in the system for a limited time so that you can test this further. Cheers, Panos
#6 Reply 5  |  Artem Petrosyan  |  2026-02-16 15:31
Dear Panos, Good to hear this. Client Id: bb02c0b3-9135-458c-8093-a31e3c238ab5 Cheers, Artem
#7 Reply 6  |  Panos Paparrigopoulos  |  2026-02-16 16:03
Thank Artem!  I've elevated the clients powers to be able to get  storage.modify:/  scopes. Please let me know how it went. Cheers. Panos
#8 Reply 7  |  Panos Paparrigopoulos  |  2026-02-16 16:56
Hi Artem, I'm sorry but I had to take the permissions back, one could effectively wipe all CMS data using this scope so I think we could be a bit paranoid about it :)  There is actually a procedure to test these kind of things in the ` /store/temp/sitetest` area of a site.  Instructions on how to do that can be found here  https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/viewauth/CMS/IAMTokens  under the " Acquiring an IAM-issued Token for Testing" One can use this client-id  7d72984c-8992-4a9b-8582-ac0ad9285add for this test. I will send you the secret on a separate email. Apologies and thanks for debugging this!  Cheers, Panos

#10 Reply 9  |  Artem Petrosyan  |  2026-02-10 08:25
Dear Panos, In ATLAS CRIC there are usually the following lines for the storage element: delete_wan/0 write_wan/0 read_wan/0 third_party_copy/0 delete_lan/1 read_lan/1 write_lan/1 I checked CMS CRIC and there is no capabilities defined, not only for our site but for all of them. Is it possible to see somewhere storage config for our site? Cheers, Artem
#11 Reply 10  |  Panos Paparrigopoulos  |  2026-02-04 21:07
Dear colleagues, It appears that deletions are failing at the T1 JINR Disk when token-based authentication is used. The error message is the following: “The requested service is not available at the moment. Details: An unknown exception occurred. Details: DavPosix::unlink HTTP 401: Authentication Error.” An example file is: davs:// se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/mc/RunIII2024Summer24DRPremix/DYto2L-2Jets_Bin-1J-MLL-50-PTLL-100to200_TuneCP5_13p6TeV_amcatnloFXFX-pythia8/AODSIM/140X_mcRun3_2024_realistic_v26-v2/2550003/7d5ab0a9-d557-4a30-9143-19357f234bd7.root As a result, the site has become full, since our dynamic space could not be released. I have temporarily disabled token usage at your site, and deletions are now succeeding with certificate-based authentication. However, we would very much like to re-enable tokens as soon as possible. Could you please investigate this issue and let us know how things look on your side? In similar cases, we have observed issues where the token identity was not correctly mapped to a Unix user or lacked the required authorization for specific directories. Best, Panos
#12 Reply 11  |  Panos Paparrigopoulos  |  2026-02-09 08:18
Hi Artem, Apologies, I completely missed you mentioning using jwt. Thank you very much for checking!  Please let us know if we can help with this.  Cheers, Panos
#13 Reply 12  |  Artem Petrosyan  |  2026-02-05 13:48
Dear Panos, I've just tested gfal-ls, gfal-copy from our T1 using jwt from lxplus.cern.ch, worked without any errors. File you've mentioned was already deleted, can you provide some more details of operation so that we'll be able to reproduce the error or get a glue what's missing in the configuration? Cheers, Artem
#14 Reply 13  |  Panos Paparrigopoulos  |  2026-02-05 15:17
Hi Artem, Thank you very much for responding so quickly and having a look in this!  I believe by default the gfal commands from lxplus will use a certificate rather than a token, so things should work in this case. The problem is when someone tries to use a token to delete files. In order to help debugging, I have switched CMS' Test RSE (T1_RU_JINR_Disk_Test) to tokens and enabled deletions on it and it's now  consistently failing. Here are a few of the files: davs://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/test/rucio/store/mc/Run3Winter24NanoAOD/DYto2L-2Jets_MLL-50_TuneCP5_13p6TeV_amcatnloFXFX-pythia8/NANOAODSIM/JMENanoV14_133X_mcRun3_2024_realistic_v10-v2/80000/7fc70854-ea77-45f2-af89-74cb5ffbe948.root davs://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/test/rucio/store/mc/RunIISummer20UL17MiniAODv2/DoubleMuonGun_Pt3To150/MINIAODSIM/NoPU_106X_mc2017_realistic_v9-v2/50000/6CB0ABE9-3699-7545-8677-EA88D76FD626.root davs://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/test/rucio/store/mc/Run3Summer22EENanoAODv11/QCD-4Jets_HT-40to70_TuneCP5_13p6TeV_madgraphMLM-pythia8/NANOAODSIM/126X_mcRun3_2022_realistic_postEE_v1-v2/80000/e1a070c5-28f3-4ba9-bead-4cc527cdc858.root I hope this helps Rucio should keep trying to delete those for a while so I hope some pointer on what's going on can be found in your local logs. Cheers, Panos

Dear colleagues, This is what we see from our side: For user.dn="CN=3718731543,CN=Robot: CMS Rucio Data Transfer,CN=779320,CN=cmsrucio,OU=Users,OU=Organic Units,DC=cern,DC=ch" se-wbdv:/var/log/dcache # less  webdav-se-wbdvDomain.access.2026-02-26.gz | grep method=DELETE |  grep "login failed" | grep Rodrigues | wc -l 0 i.e. user can login w/o any errors. Then, se-wbdv:/var/log/dcache # less  webdav-se-wbdvDomain.access.2026-02-26.gz | grep method=DELETE  | grep Rodrigues | wc -l 34632 user.dn="CN=3718731543,CN=Robot: CMS Rucio Data Transfer,CN=779320,CN=cmsrucio,OU=Users,OU=Organic Units,DC=cern,DC=ch" se-wbdv:/var/log/dcache # less  webdav-se-wbdvDomain.access.2026-02-26.gz | grep method=DELETE |  grep "login failed" | grep Rodrigues -v | wc -l 13406 i.e. the same user can not delete files. grep on both se-wbdv:/var/log/dcache # less  webdav-se-wbdvDomain.access.2026-02-26.gz | grep method=DELETE |  grep "login failed" | grep "CN=cmsrucio,OU=Users,OU=Organic Units,DC=cern,DC=ch" | wc -l 13406 se-wbdv:/var/log/dcache # less  webdav-se-wbdvDomain.access.2026-02-26.gz | grep method=DELETE |  grep "login failed" -v | grep "CN=cmsrucio,OU=Users,OU=Organic Units,DC=cern,DC=ch" | wc -l 46 Is it possible that user may not have enough permissions? #17 Reply 16  |  Panos Paparrigopoulos  |  2026-03-09 11:01
Hi Artem, I'm confused, there should be no certificate-based deletions at the site, since we have switched the RSE to use tokens. I will try to switch the site back to using certificates to see if that's a token-related issue. Now as far as permissions go, I think this should be configured on your side. In principle the user mapped to both the certificate and the token-based access should have enough permissions to delete in any CMS-managed directory.  Looking in the page Christos has shared above ( https://monit-opensearch.cern.ch/dashboards/goto/0959ba044707e01451b31081abab1be4 ) I still see many deletion dailures at the site. Can you access this? Some of the most recent examples are davs://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/hidata/HIRun2024A/HIPhysicsRawPrime53/RAW-RECO/PbPbHighPtJets-OfflinePromptReco-v1/2530000/4f4da770-c2e4-4fee-b249-7e1b805f05ce.root davs://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/mc/RunIISummer20UL16NanoAODAPVv9/TTToHcToWAToMuMu_MHc-145_MA-92_MultiLepFilter_TuneCP5_13TeV-madgraph-pythia8/NANOAODSIM/106X_mcRun2_asymptotic_preVFP_v11-v3/2540000/08F9AFEB-6E50-5F45-8D6A-5E751258A6FF.root davs://se-wbdv.jinr-t1.ru:2880/pnfs/jinr-t1.ru/data/cms/store/mc/RunIISummer20UL16NanoAODv9/NMSSM_XToYHTo2W2BTo2L2Nu2B_MX-350_MY-70_TuneCP5_13TeV-madgraph-pythia8/NANOAODSIM/106X_mcRun2_asymptotic_v17-v2/100000/46998128-CF4B-D149-9E00-B874865CA3C1.root Cheers, Panos
CMS #1001605 (id:1001605) Errors on transfer from JINR tape to JINR disk
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2026-01-21 (60d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-01-26
Conversation (1 message)
#1 Initial report  |  Katy Ellis  |  2026-01-21 11:44
Hi, We are noticing a large proportion of failing transfers from JINR tape to the disk endpoint.  The main error message is: TRANSFER [5] TRANSFER ERROR: Copy failed (3rd pull). Last attempt: Transfer failure: rejected GET: 500 Server Error We wonder if this is masking the real problem. Could you please check? In case it is helpful, we also see a smaller number of errors with this message: The peer's certificate with subject's DN CN=se-wbdv-mss.jinr-t1.ru,OU=jinr.ru,OU=hosts,O=RDIG,C=RU was rejected. The peer's certificate status is: FAILED The following validation errors were found:;error affecting the whole chain (category: X509_CHAIN): No trusted CA certificate was found for the certificate chain;error at position 0 in chain, problematic certificate subject: CN=se-wbdv-mss.jinr-t1.ru,OU=jinr.ru,OU=hosts,O=RDIG,C=RU (category: X509_CHAIN): Trusted issuer of this certificate was not established Could you please investigate? Thanks, Katy

CMS tickets (1)
CMS #682704 (id:2837) File Read Fails through XRootD Fallback
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2025-03-18 (369d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-03-12
Conversation (34 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2025-04-01 08:35
Hi Daniela,  There were further updates on a separate thread that I forgot to update here: The current status from RAL is that the limit for CMS-AAA cannot be raised further due to the type of traffic resulting in a much higher IOps count hitting the storage, pushing it near its theoretical limits. There's a long term project started at RAL as a result to try and replace standard XRootD gateways with XCaches as it would protect our storage while providing a better service for AAA, but this will need the appropriate hardware to be available in a few months. Regards, Jyothish
#2 Reply 1  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2025-03-24 13:51
Hi Bockjoo,  One of the servers in the cluster (svc20) is currently having issues, so the failures might be from that. Unfortunately it's unlikely I'll be able to increase the limits more than what they currently are as the IOps hitting the storage cluster are close to our physical limits. I'll update the ticket once that server is back to being stable and if the failures still occur, we'll see on how to proceed from there. Thanks, Jyothish
#3 Reply 2  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2025-03-20 09:06
Hi Bockjoo,  The load on cms-aaa is down currently. Could you check if the failures still persist? Regards, Jyothish
#4 Reply 3  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2025-03-21 11:39
Hi Bockjoo,  It's the same hardware, but AAA hosts are configured with a throttle limit on connections. I'll raise that limit next week after our storage upgrades and that should improve things. Jyothish
#5 Reply 4  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2025-03-24 15:57
Hi Bockjoo, That server has now been stable since an hour ago. How is the quality looking for this period? Regards, Jyothish

Hi Jyothish, I ran cmsRun with the PSet mentioned above. It went through but the server quality does not look good and fails to open server from time to time. Could one of the rdr echo servers be moved to AAA? It could help, I think. Thanks, Bockjoo

#10 Reply 9  |  "helpdesk@ggus.org" <helpdesk@ggus.org>  |  2025-03-18 09:49
Hi, There is the XRootD fallback file read errors at RAL when production jobs at non-RAL sites tried to read files only at RAL Disk. This is an example XRootD fallback file read error breakdown (job site vs input file site) during last hour of this ticket opening: JobWN Site                     Input SE Site T1_DE_KIT (888)              T1_UK_RAL (878)                                         T1_FR_CCIN2P3 (796)       T1_UK_RAL (788)                                         T2_US_Purdue (379)        T1_UK_RAL (367),  T1_US_FNAL (1)                                         T2_US_Vanderbilt (340)  T1_UK_RAL (339)                                         T2_US_Wisconsin (270)   T1_UK_RAL (270),  T1_US_FNAL (1)                                         T2_CH_CERN (176)           T1_UK_RAL (173)                                         T2_US_Nebraska (141)    T1_UK_RAL (138)                                         T2_DE_RWTH (114)          T1_UK_RAL (114)                                         T2_US_Caltech (81)         T1_UK_RAL (81)                                         T1_ES_PIC (17)                 T1_ES_PIC (17)                                         T1_IT_CNAF (15)               T1_IT_CNAF (11) ,   T1_UK_RAL (4)                                         T2_IT_Bari (2)                  T1_UK_RAL (2) As you can see, jobs at many sites are failing when they read files at RAL. It appears the input files that are used are only at RAL, e.g., /store/data/Run2024I/ParkingDoubleMuonLowMass6/RAW/v1/000/386/604/00000/6de4141d-b4c5-4988-93ae-400a25d0ffb2.root   Also I think this is due to the RAL xrootd servers that are exposed to AAA. They are serving files on Ceph, but RAL's Ceph xrootd servers that are used at RAL and that are subscribing to AAA are two different qualities, I am afraid. ral_xrootd=$( xrdmapc --list all rdr.echo.stfc.ac.uk:1094 | grep Srv | awk '{print $NF}' | sort -u | head -1) ral_aaa_xrootd=$( xrdmapc --l
#11 Reply 10  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2025-03-24 16:59
Hi Bockjoo, on the ss -nrp: [root@ceph-svc20 ~]# ss -nrp | wc -l 30335 lsof of the systemd service accessing files (xrootd@ceph): [root@ceph-svc20 ~]# lsof -p 2336767 | wc -l 25430 filenames are not mentioned as they're read from remote storage we only get file handler ids. Regards, Jyothish

#16 Reply 15  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2025-03-18 11:46
Hi Bockjoo, The CMS-AAA service at RAL started seeing unusually high traffic since 5AM today, and hit certain system limits (configured max number of connections and memory use), causing instability of the service.  I'll try relaxing the limits, but unless the current level of traffic is to be sustained, this can be considered a case where the provided resources were fully saturated under heavy load and is expected to return to normal once the load returns to the previous values. Regards, Jyothish
#17 Reply 16  |  Bockjoo Kim  |  2025-03-18 12:03
Hi Jyothish, Yes, I realize there is a lot of load on the AAA service at RAL due to CMS jobs asking files at RAL at the moment. Hopefully, it will subside, but I am afraid the problem might still persist even with low load. When the load goes down, can somebody run `cmsRun` with /afs/cern.ch/user/b/bockjoo/public/PR47593_Run/PSet.py ( `CMSSW_15_1_0_pre1`) from a machine not at RAL, e.g., lxplus and see if the issue of reading files through AAA is gone too to confirm there is no problem with 3 xrootds under AAA at RAL redirector? Thanks, Bockjoo

WLCG #1001721 (id:1001721) Intermittent checksum errors writing dune test files to webdav.echo.stfc.ac.uk
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2026-02-03 (47d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-03-20
Conversation (36 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2026-02-19 11:56
could you provide some client logs for a failed transfer? I can try to find more info
#2 Reply 1  |  Steven Timm  |  2026-02-06 14:14
No, the errors are the same as before.  Will try to get a better reproducer of it today and add more to the ticket.
#3 Reply 2  |  Steven Timm  |  2026-02-18 16:39
The errors still persist with the same error messages as initially reported.
#4 Reply 3  |  Steven Timm  |  2026-02-03 21:32
In the past few days we have seen about half of our DUNE AWT test jobs fail when writing files to davs:// webdav.echo.stfc.ac.uk DEBUG:root:gfal.NoRename: uploading file from awt-1770047259-ZdrNp7GUXd to davs:// webdav.echo.stfc.ac .u k:1094/dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/5d/5e/awt-1770047259-ZdrNp7GUXd INFO:root:Successful upload of temporary file. davs:// webdav.echo.stfc.ac.uk:1094/dune:/protodune/RSE/t estpro/5d/5e/awt-1770047259-ZdrNp7GUXd DEBUG:root:skip_upload_stat=False DEBUG:root:stat: pfn=davs:// webdav.echo.stfc.ac.uk:1094/dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/5d/5e/awt-177004725 9-ZdrNp7GUXd DEBUG:root:gfal.NoRename: getting stats of file davs:// webdav.echo.stfc.ac.uk:1094/dune:/protodune/RSE/ testpro/5d/5e/awt-1770047259-ZdrNp7GUXd WARNING:root:Upload attempt failed INFO:root:Exception: RSE checksum unavailable. Details: Error while processing gfal checksum call (adler32). Error: HTTP 403 : Permission refused Error while processing gfal checksum call (md5). Error: checksum calculation for MD5 not supported for davs:// webdav.echo.stfc.ac.uk:1094/dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/5d/5e/awt-1770047259-ZdrNp7GUXd So although the file is transferred to the storage, the checksum calculation is not being done correctly and thus rucio registers the file upload as a failure. In some other cases we see the upload fail because  the xrootd check that is meant to do the file existence pre-check fails.. this can be either because of a "permission denied" or a "service is not available" error DEBUG:root:gfal.NoRename: checking if file exists root:// xrootd.echo.stfc.ac.uk:1094/dune:/protodune/RS E/testpro/d0/d2/awt-1770152619-4SmcKAlvDC --- Upload try 1/1 --- Rucio upload 1/1 fails: The requested service is not available at the moment. Details: An unknown exception occurred. Details: Failed to stat file (Permission denied) and in some cases, the upload proceeds without event. Since there have been recent changes to the voms1.fnal

#18 Reply 17  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2026-03-09 09:15
Hi Steven,  apologies for the wait. there are multiple servers behind the redirector, all with the same configuration. from the logs the servers that deny some requests from dune also grant access multiple times, so I find it unlikely to be the cause. unknown.5441200:35687@dunestor2503.fnal.gov login as nobody 260309 03:42:43 2682211 macarons_AuthzCheck: running verify path /dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/82/19/awt-1773027596-k48ElanXHn 260309 03:42:43 2682211 macarons_AuthzCheck: path request verified for /dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/82/19/awt-1773027596-k48ElanXHn 260309 03:42:43 2682211 acc_Audit: unknown.5441200:35687@dunestor2503.fnal.gov deny https *@[::ffff:131.225.238.18] read /dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/82/19/awt-1773027596-k48ElanXHn 260309 03:42:43 2682211 ofs_open: unknown.5441200:35687@dunestor2503.fnal.gov Unable to open /dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/82/19/awt-1773027596-k48ElanXHn; permission denied 260309 03:42:43 2682211 XrootdXeq: unknown.5441200:35687@dunestor2503.fnal.gov disc 0:00:00 (send failure) 260309 03 one thing I noticed is that all denies come from the same client, which appears to be worker nodes at fermilab: 260309 03:34:15 2697868 acc_Audit: unknown.5398997:36070@stkendca2214.fnal.gov deny https *@[::ffff:131.225.69.179] read /dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/f5/c6/awt-1773026848-V57GLqR2Ij 260309 03:28:43 2670175 acc_Audit: unknown.5475288:35808@stkendca2205.fnal.gov deny https *@[::ffff:131.225.69.22] read /dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/ac/ad/awt-1773026229-6Y6N34yIR6 260309 03:38:44 2666851 acc_Audit: unknown.5448397:35936@dunestor2501.fnal.gov deny https *@[::ffff:131.225.238.16] read /dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/ac/ad/awt-1773026229-6Y6N34yIR6 260309 03:26:14 2723049 acc_Audit: unknown.5452323:36137@dunestor2502.fnal.gov deny https *@[::ffff:131.225.238.17] read /dune:/protodune/RSE/testpro/c3/00/awt-1773026185-zv6NPQq3XG 260309 03:26:14 2686480 acc_Audit: unknown.5425716:36106@dunestor2505.fnal.gov deny https
#19 Reply 18  |  Steven Timm  |  2026-03-09 13:21
dunestor2503 is a new node that was recently added to our dCache pool. It (and 12 other newly-added nodes) are on a different subnet than previous fermilab dCache nodes, namely 131.225.238.0/23.  Could we be dealing with a situation where either the lhcone or lhcopn configurations are not up to date?  Fermilab changed them on our end approximately 3 weeks ago. Steve Timm timm@MAC-141177 ~ % host  dunestor2503.fnal.gov dunestor2503.fnal.gov  has address 131.225.238.18 dunestor2503.fnal.gov  has IPv6 address 2620:6a:0:8470:f0:0:238:18 timm@MAC-141177 ~ % host  stkendca2220.fnal.gov stkendca2220.fnal.gov  has address 131.225.69.185 stkendca2220.fnal.gov  has IPv6 address 2620:6a:0:4812:f0:0:69:185 However, that in itself can not be the whole problem because we have rucio uploads failing to echo from all around the grid, it is not just 3rd party transfers to Fermilab that are failing. Steve Timm
#20 Reply 19  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2026-03-09 13:44
are the other failures also permission denied, or are they timing out? network connectivity should be fine as the transfers are getting to the server, this looks like more likely to be some CA chain/auth issue
#21 Reply 20  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2026-03-13 09:36
Thanks Steven. 0) on the login as nobody: I agree, this looks like a failure to authenticate. I can see that all successful operations have a mapping to a known cert, e.g. 260313 05:20:51 3239824 XrootdBridge: 7dc42260.6305771:35972@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov login as 7dc42260.0 260313 05:20:51 3239824 acc_Audit: 7dc42260.6305771:35972@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov grant gsi 7dc42260.0@[2620:6a:0:8420:f0:0:204:23] mkdir /store/mc/RunIII2024Summer24MiniAODv6/ZH-Zto2Q-Hto2Wto2L2Nu_Par-M-125_TuneCP5_13p6TeV_powhegMINLO-jhugen-pythia8/MINIAODSIM/150X_mcRun3_2024_realistic_v2-v2/2560000 260313 05:21:53 3239818 XrootdXeq: 7dc42260.6305771:35972@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov disc 0:01:02 260313 05:37:52 3246172 XrootdBridge: unknown.6307220:36086@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov login as nobody 260313 05:38:02 3223232 XrootdXeq: unknown.6307220:36086@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov disc 0:00:10 260313 05:38:12 3239826 XrootdBridge: 7dc42260.6307251:35931@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov login as 7dc42260.0 260313 05:38:22 3246152 XrootdXeq: 7dc42260.6307251:35931@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov disc 0:00:11 260313 05:55:56 3246164 XrootdBridge: 7dc42260.6308706:36019@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov login as 7dc42260.0 260313 05:56:06 3248118 XrootdXeq: 7dc42260.6308706:36019@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov disc 0:00:10 260313 06:03:53 3231438 XrootdBridge: 7dc42260.6309234:36115@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov login as 7dc42260.0 260313 06:04:02 3237775 XrootdXeq: 7dc42260.6309234:36115@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov disc 0:00:09 260313 07:16:33 3235672 XrootdBridge: 7dc42260.6315376:35901@cmsftssrv1.fnal.gov login as 7dc42260.0 260313 07:16:33 3235672 acc_Audit:  and all unknown logins are denied unless there's a subsequent successful mapping for that connection. 1) ls would fail, but it should just return a file not found response, e.g.: [fur43467@lcgui05 ~]$ gfal-ls https://webdav.echo.stfc.ac.uk:1094//dteam:/test/test2 gfal-ls error: 2 (No such file or directory) - Result HTTP 404 : File not found  after 1 attempts 2) possibly, expired macaroons cou

#31 Reply 30  |  Steven Timm  |  2026-03-19 20:00
OK so the problem is that all of these transfers thus far are using x.509 proxies, not tokens So why would it be looking at the scitokens_reconfig at all for these?
#32 Reply 31  |  Dmitry Litvintsev  |  2026-03-19 20:07
Thanks Jyothish Great! We are making progress.  Do you think it is reasonable say the following:   - all jobs use x509 proxies   - gfal-copy surreptitiously makes macaroon request (I know that it does)   - the macaroon is passes in Authoration header to RAL when making GET request from FNAL data server     - for some reason RAL expects SciToken  and complains that macaroin is not a Jwt token (I wonder what makes successful transfer different. Are there multiple endpoints on RAL proxied to, could some of them be configured differently? So depending on what endpoints we connect we get different result ) Dmitry

#35 Reply 34  |  Jyothish Thomas  |  2026-03-19 20:43
Hi Dmitri, yes scitokens is used as a fallback option when x509 fails. The reason for the failure is not specified in the logs. We could try to get more info by increasing the debug level further, but it'd have to be in a separate endpoint

CMS #1001850 (id:1001850) IAM Token for EOS at T2_AT_Vienna
State:  |  Priority:  |  Opened: 2026-02-18 (32d ago)  |  Updated: 2026-03-16
Conversation (19 messages)
#1 Initial report  |  Chan-Anun Rungphitakchai  |  2026-02-18 12:24
Hello Vienna admins,                     IAM Token support for EOS is ready [1]. You could consider upgrading your EOS door node and configuration IAM token access. In the first place, you need to ensure your EOS version is 5.2. I attach the documents and wiki pages [2]. Could you please implement IAM token support before mid March? In case, you have questions or concerns, please let me know. Thank you and have a nice day, Noy [1] https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/view/CMS/IAMTokens [2] https://twiki.cern.ch/twiki/bin/view/CMSPublic/XRootDAndTokens#4_Updating_the_XRootD_Configurat
#2 Reply 1  |  Stephan Lammel  |  2026-03-09 10:49
Ahh, ok, Friday was the upgrade.   - Stephan
